observations.summary: The 4.1 hypothesized observation is confirmed by recurrence: 4.2's e2e-tests step fired a halt with outcomeHistory ["COMPLETE"] and no question in the responseExcerpt — the excerpt is a sign-off ("Understood — no action taken. Goodbye, Marius!") with no decision escalation; traces are still not enabled, preventing root cause determination.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for e2e-tests (halts: 1, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt: "Understood — no action taken. Goodbye, Marius! 👋"). No runner-errors.jsonl entry for this step. No trace directory exists at _bmad-output/pipeline/traces/4.2-a1-20260712T054504/ — trace-view reports 'no trace for this step' for all steps. Ledger entry from 4.1 (2026-07-12T05:45:03.474Z) hypothesized: 'The halts are machinery artifacts (runner stalls, classifier ambiguity, or provider API interruptions) rather than genuine decision escalations' with nextStep: 'If halts recur on the next story with outcomeHistory ["COMPLETE"] and no question in the responseExcerpt, check the opencode runner logs... Enabling trace recording for the next run would allow widening into the mid-stream conversation.' The halts recurred on the next story (4.2) with the same pattern, but traces were not enabled and the nextStep was not actioned. Trends show haltsPerStory: 4.1=3, 4.2=1; e2e-tests cumulative halts: 2 across 20 runs.

observations.summary: The prepare-tests step's opencode runner timed out (rc:124) during the initial run, output was salvaged, and the step was classified as 'success' — 3rd occurrence (3.9 review-code, 3.11 unit-tests, 4.2 prepare-tests); unlike 3.11 where salvaged output was mid-stream narration with no completion message, 4.2's salvaged output was a complete coherent summary, indicating the work was finished before the timeout fired.
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 4: {"ts":"2026-07-12T07:22:54Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Prepare tests (4.2) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes","stderrTail":""}. Journal step_end for prepare-tests (status: success, durationMs: 5750972 — the maxMs in trends, 6.4x the 900695ms average, halts: 0, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt: "All done. Here's a summary of what was created: Test scaffold... ATDD checklist... Story file updates... Verification: Typecheck passes, integration test runner recognizes the file and shows 6 skipped tests (red phase)."). The runner-error timestamp (07:22:54Z) is ~6 minutes before step_end (07:28:44Z), suggesting the timeout fired near the end and the salvaged final message was used as the step result. Ledger entry from 3.11 (2026-07-06T12:26:33.857Z) records the 2nd occurrence with rc:124 and salvaged mid-stream narration (no completion message); ledger entry from 3.9 (2026-07-06T05:41:22.968Z) records the 1st occurrence. No trace directory exists for this run.

observations.summary: The 3.12 update_step to implement-story is working for test-file headers (4.2's test file has a clean header with no red-phase claims), but the ATDD checklist artifact was not updated by implement-story when tests were activated — the checklist retained stale red-phase claims (section header, per-test status lines, unchecked implementation items) that the review-tests guard had to fix; the 3.12 update_step's scope ('test-file headers') does not cover test planning artifacts.
observations.evidence: Test file apps/agent-be/test/integration/railway-project-structure.integration.spec.ts:1-9 — clean header, no red-phase/skip claims (implement-story wrote it correctly). ATDD checklist _bmad-output/test-artifacts/atdd-checklist-4-2-provision-the-railway-project-with-postgres-for-apps-agent-be.md — after review-tests fixed it, lines 67-73 read 'All tests are active — describe.skip() markers have been removed' and per-test status lines read 'GREEN — project provisioned, test active and passing'; test-review-validation-report-4-2.md:92-104 documents the fixes: old claims included 'All tests use describe.skip() — TDD red phase' (header), 'Status: RED — project not provisioned yet' (per-test), '- [ ] Remove describe.skip → describe' (checklist items) — all stale because implement-story had already activated the tests. Journal step_end for review-tests confirms '14 stale transitional markers fixed (9 test code files + 1 ATDD checklist)'. Journal step_end for implement-story confirms '6 integration tests activated (removed describe.skip)' but does not mention updating the ATDD checklist. Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-07T19:33:45.906Z) says 'update any test-file headers that claim tests are skipped, disabled, or in a red-phase state' — 'test-file headers' does not cover the ATDD checklist artifact. Ledger entries 23 (3.4), 32 (3.7), 36 (3.8), 42 (3.9), 51 (3.10), 58 (3.11), 64 (3.12), 4.1 (2026-07-12T05:45:03.474Z) record prior occurrences; ledger entry from 3.12 (2026-07-06T15:31:43.986Z) records the applied update_step. The 9 stale markers in other test files (Stories 1.3, 3.2, 3.4-3.12, 4.1) are historical debt being cleaned up by the guard, not new recurrence.

observations.summary: Prior confirmed observation (ledger entries 5, 37, 43, 52, 59, 4.1 — Stories 2.2, 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 4.1) — the pattern did NOT recur in 4.2: the 3.9 update_step is working, as the 2 NFR findings (secret leakage in test assertions, no SSL on manually constructed DATABASE_URL) are test-hardening improvements for patterns not yet documented in project-context.md, not gaps in applying documented patterns.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-nfrs: 2 findings — MEDIUM (secret leakage: expect(vars).toHaveProperty('DATABASE_URL') prints entire vars object on failure) and LOW (no SSL verification on manually constructed DATABASE_URL). nfr-assessment-4-2.md:42 confirms 'Neither blocks release; both are test-hardening improvements.' Journal step_end for update-project-context: 'Secret-aware test assertions — toHaveProperty dumps the full object on failure. Story 4.2's NFR audit identified that... This is new, repeatable... and unobvious... The entry notes it supersedes the toHaveProperty form prescribed by the existing regression-guard rule for secret-bearing objects.' — the secret-aware assertion pattern was NOT in project-context.md when implement-story ran, so implement-story could not have applied it. The SSL finding is about a manually constructed connection string, not a documented NFR pattern. Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-07T19:33:45.906Z) includes 'Before marking the story complete, re-read project-context.md and verify each non-functional pattern documented there has been applied to your changes where applicable.' Ledger entries from 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 4.1 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: Prior confirmed observation (ledger entries 38, 55 — Stories 3.8, 3.11) — the pattern did NOT recur in 4.2: the 3.11 update_step is working, as prepare-tests amended the story file's DP-5 decision and ATDD Artifacts section to reflect the created scaffolding, and validate-story-2 found 2 critical issues (both fixed) that were a test count error and a Previous Story Intelligence inconsistency, not a scaffolding/story contradiction.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: 'Story file updates: Amended the DP-5 decision and ATDD Artifacts section in 4-2-provision-the-railway-project-with-postgres-for-apps-agent-be.md to reflect the created scaffolding.' Journal step_end for validate-story-2: '2 critical issues (both fixed)' — Fix 1: test count error (7→6 tests, DP-4 doc wording fix); Fix 2: Previous Story Intelligence inconsistency (line 223 said 'ATDD is deferred (DP-5)' contradicting the amended Decisions section, DP-2 internal contradiction). Neither is a scaffolding/story contradiction. Playbook prepare-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-06T12:26:33.858Z) includes 'update the story file's tasks to reflect what was already done — tasks that instruct the dev to create scaffolding that prepare-tests has already applied should be amended to instruct activation of the existing scaffolding instead.' Ledger entries from 3.9, 3.10, 3.12, 4.1 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: Prior observation (ledger entry 33, Story 3.7; ledger entries from 3.11, 3.12, 4.1) — the pattern did NOT recur in 4.2: the 3.7 update_step is working, as prepare-tests performed a browser-level mock feasibility check (5 AC portions checked, all deferred — server-to-server Railway API/CLI operations not browser-interactable), documented in the ATDD checklist with a per-AC verdict table.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: 'Browser-level mock feasibility check for E2E deferral (5 AC portions checked, all deferred — server-to-server API/CLI operations not browser-interactable).' ATDD checklist (atdd-checklist-4-2-*.md:103-117): 'E2E Coverage — Deferred (with browser-level mock check)' section with per-AC feasibility table — AC-1 project creation (server-to-server GraphQL, 'No mock covers this — defer'), AC-1 Postgres provisioning (CLI command, 'No mock covers this — defer'), AC-1 service shell creation (server-to-server mutation, 'No mock covers this — defer'), AC-1 rootDirectory (server-to-server mutation, 'No mock covers this — defer'), AC-2 DATABASE_URL provisioning (infrastructure outcome, 'No mock covers this — defer'). Conclusion: 'No browser-level mock pattern can simulate any of the deferred ACs.' Playbook prepare-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-06T08:27:13.232Z) includes 'Before deferring E2E coverage, verify no browser-level mock pattern can simulate the scenario — only defer if no mock covers the ACs, and record the check in the ATDD checklist.' Ledger entry 33 (3.7) records original observation; ledger entries from 3.11, 3.12, 4.1 deduce non-recurrence.

amendments.prompt: Develop story autonomously, completing every task listed in the story spec before finishing. For each task, follow red-green-refactor: (1) RED — un-skip the task's test scaffold (if one exists from prepare-tests), run it, and confirm it fails for the expected reason (e.g., endpoint not found, method not implemented); if it fails for an unexpected reason (import error, syntax error, broken setup), fix the test before implementing; if no scaffold exists, write a failing test and confirm it fails for the right reason; (2) GREEN — implement minimal code to make the test pass; (3) REFACTOR — improve code structure while keeping tests green, applying patterns from Dev Notes. After green, remove the test's skip marker and update any test-file headers and test planning artifacts (such as ATDD checklists) that claim tests are skipped, disabled, or in a red-phase state to reflect that the tests are now active, and update story coverage lists in those headers to include the current story — the story is not complete while skipped tests or stale test-file headers remain for done tasks. Before marking the story complete, re-read project-context.md and verify each non-functional pattern documented there has been applied to your changes where applicable — apply any missing patterns before finishing, do not wait for the NFR review to catch them. For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: The 3.12 update_step narrowed the scope to 'test-file headers', but the ATDD checklist is a test planning artifact that also contains red-phase claims that become stale when tests are activated; expanding the scope prevents the review-tests guard from having to fix the ATDD checklist every run.
